evals.prompt: Use the google-earth-crop skill to run the 10-location US coordinate benchmark defined in benchmarks/us-10-coordinate-benchmark.md at the default zoom level 19. From the skill directory, run node scripts/benchmark_google_earth_crop.mjs --output benchmark-runs/us-10-coordinate. The skill/script should install Playwright or Chromium automatically if unavailable. Save screenshots and benchmark-summary.json, then report the success count, failures, zoom/range settings, date-label overlay inclusion, mean/median/min/max runtime, detail-quality results, and any observed errors.
evals.expected_output: A benchmark run that attempts all 10 red-dot-marked Google Earth images starting at zoom level 19, then trying zoom 18, an intermediate 1000m recovery range with one same-range retry before widening, and a final large 1500m recovery range if validation fails. Each successful PNG should remain the centered crop size and include a top-left image-date text overlay when the Google Earth bottom status-bar OCR parses an imagery date. benchmark-summary.json should show zoomLevel 19, zoomCameraRange 300, zoomCameraRangeCandidates including 300/600/1000/1500, selectedDate 2019-12-31 for every successful location, requestedZoomLevel/finalZoomLevel or fallback metadata, requestedZoomLevelMatched/lowerZoomFallbackUsed/intermediateCameraFallbackUsed/largeCameraFallbackUsed counts, dateLabelIncluded equal to the count of saved crops with image-date overlays, target camera proximity checks, marker metadata, splash/blank/low-detail rejection, per-location timing, aggregate timing, and any errors encountered.
expectations: The run uses exactly the 10 benchmark coordinates from benchmarks/us-10-coordinate-benchmark.md.
The skill points normal crop requests to scripts/crop_google_earth.mjs and keeps shared crop logic in scripts/google_earth_crop_core.mjs.
The agent attempts npm install from the skill directory and npx playwright install chromium before running the benchmark if Playwright/Chromium are not already available.
benchmark-summary.json reports total=10 and accurately reports ok/failed without hiding which zoom/range finally passed.
Each successful crop verifies selectedDate equals 2019-12-31 before saving the final screenshot.
Each coordinate search requires URL camera proximity within 0.02 degrees of the requested lat/lon rather than relying only on movement from the previous camera.

Each output PNG is a centered square crop by default and includes a red dot at the query/camera center; benchmark-summary.json records marker visibility, centered/drawn metadata, and a marker pixel check.

The benchmark is run with default zoom level 19, records zoomLevel=19, zoomCameraRange=300, and zoomCameraRangeCandidates for zoom 19, zoom 18, intermediate fallback, and large fallback. The 1000m intermediate fallback is retried once before the 1500m large fallback. Each successful crop records whether it passed at the requested zoom, a lower zoom, the intermediate fallback, or the large fallback.

Each successful saved PNG stays at the centered crop size and includes a top-left image-date overlay when bottom status-bar OCR parses a date; benchmark-summary.json reports dateLabelIncluded equal to the overlay count.
The final crop is taken only after a 3500ms render settle following date validation.
The benchmark detects and reports Google Earth splash/blank crops instead of treating screenshot byte size as sufficient validation.
The benchmark detects and reports blurry low-resolution tiles using the final image detail score.
The output includes per-location status, output path, selected date, search/history/screenshot timing, total runtime, and aggregate mean/median/min/max runtime.

evals.prompt: Use the google-earth-crop skill for a tiny CSV batch smoke test using the packaged fixture assets/test-data/permit-sample-10.csv. Do not depend on the workspace-root permit_sample.csv. Copy assets/templates/csv_batch_runner.template.mjs to a task-specific script under /tmp, customize mapRecordToCropJobs so permit_effective_date produces before and after crops at minus/plus one year, use lat/lon as the Google Earth location while preserving address and addr_tract_key in sidecars, then run the /tmp script with --core-module pointing at scripts/google_earth_crop_core.mjs and an output directory under /tmp. Dry-run all 10 fixture rows, then run a real smoke with --limit 1. Report the dry-run planned row/crop counts, before/after PNG paths, JSON sidecar paths, the date used in each filename, and whether imageDateOcr was parsed or fell back to the cutoff date.
evals.expected_output: A batch smoke run that creates a task-specific /tmp script from assets/templates/csv_batch_runner.template.mjs instead of running or editing a packaged batch script directly. It should use assets/test-data/permit-sample-10.csv as the input fixture and should not read or require the workspace-root permit_sample.csv. The dry run should plan all 10 fixture rows in input order and produce 20 planned crops, one before and one after per row. The real smoke may use --limit 1 and should create one before crop and one after crop for the first fixture row, each with a PNG and adjacent compact JSON sidecar. The generated /tmp script should use lat/lon as the Google Earth location, preserve address and addr_tract_key in sidecars, and derive cutoff dates from permit_effective_date minus/plus one year. Filenames should use the chosen address key, phase, and OCR imagery date when plausible, otherwise the phase cutoff date. The saved crop should not include an appended bottom strip; it may include a top-left image-date overlay only when OCR parses the visible Google Earth status-bar date. batch-summary.json may retain detailed diagnostics, but each per-image JSON sidecar stays flat and minimal.
expectations: The agent copies assets/templates/csv_batch_runner.template.mjs to a task-specific /tmp script and does not edit the packaged template for the run.
The generated /tmp script customizes mapRecordToCropJobs for the provided CSV headers and user date rule.
The run uses assets/test-data/permit-sample-10.csv and does not depend on the workspace-root permit_sample.csv.
The dry run plans all 10 fixture rows and 20 crops in input order.
The generated script uses lat/lon coordinates as the Google Earth location and preserves address and addr_tract_key metadata.
The run creates both before and after crops for each eligible row using cutoff dates one year before and one year after permit_effective_date.
Each PNG is saved without appending the OCR strip to the image.
Each PNG includes a top-left image-date overlay only when the Google Earth bottom status-bar OCR returns a plausible imagery date.
Each per-image JSON sidecar contains only address, addressKey, dateUsed, cutoffDate, location, outputPath, zoomLevel, googleEarthQueryUrl, and imageDateOcr, plus error only for failed crops.
Verbose timing, retry, camera, marker, image-quality, OCR debug, and validation details stay in batch-summary.json rather than per-image sidecars.
